```markdown
---
name: gdrive-backup-export
description: >
  Export key Drive docs to local archive. Requires integrations: @google-drive, @filesystem. Uses search_tool + use_tool. Cadence: monthly. Triggers: "gdrive backup export", /gdrive-backup-export.
metadata:
  category: google-drive
  type: combo
  cadence: monthly
  risk: medium
  connectors: google-drive,filesystem
  integrations: @google-drive,@filesystem
allowed-tools: search_tool, use_tool
---
# /gdrive-backup-export

Export key Drive docs to local archive.

## Works with

Use this as a portable `SKILL.md` in Grok, Claude, Cursor, ChatGPT, Codex, VS Code agent skills, Gemini, or any AI workspace that supports reusable markdown instructions. If a tool does not support slash commands, paste the task and ask the AI to follow this skill.

## When to use

The user's critical documents live only in Drive - contracts, tax records, project deliverables - and they want a local, offline copy: a monthly archive run, an exit-backup before leaving a job or workspace, or a snapshot of one project folder before a big reorganization.

## Inputs to gather

Check context first; only ask if missing:

- What to back up: a named folder tree, files matching a query (e.g. everything in "Contracts"), or a shortlist of key docs
- Local destination path, and whether to overwrite or version (default: dated subfolder `backup-YYYY-MM-DD/`, never overwrite)
- Export formats for Google-native files: Docs → .docx or .pdf, Sheets → .xlsx or .csv, Slides → .pptx or .pdf (Google formats cannot be saved as-is; each needs a conversion choice)
- Size/skip rules: max file size, and whether to skip videos or other bulky binaries

## Tone & voice

- **Tone:** Link to files. Summarize before long reads.
- **Audience:** Infer from user context.

## Connector access (required)

This skill **cannot run from text alone** - it must call live integrations.
Before any workflow step:

1. Check your AI tool's connector settings and confirm each integration below is enabled and authenticated (Grok: run `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: enable the app in settings · other tools: ask your AI what app connections it has).
2. Use `search_tool` to discover tools for each connector (search by app name).
3. Call integrations with `use_tool` and fully-qualified names (`server__tool_name`).
4. Connector tool names vary by AI tool. Discover the available tools first, then call the exact tool name exposed in the current session.

### Required integrations

- **@google-drive** - Google Drive
  - MCP server key: `google-drive`
  - Example tools: `google-drive__search_files`, `google-drive__read_file`, `google-drive__create_file`

- **@filesystem** - Local filesystem
  - MCP server key: `filesystem`
  - Example tools: `filesystem__read_file`, `filesystem__write_file`, `filesystem__list_directory`

### If a connector is missing

Stop immediately. Tell the user:

```
This skill needs <@mention> enabled.
1. Open your AI tool's connector/integration settings (Grok: `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: app settings)
2. Enable and authenticate <app>
3. Refresh your tool list, then run this skill again
```

Do **not** pretend you accessed Gmail/Canva/Drive without a successful `use_tool` call.

## Safety

- **Propose before write**: show a review table; wait for user to say `apply`, `apply all`, or `apply 1,3`.
- Never delete, send, or publish without explicit confirmation.
- Summarize every action taken at the end of the run so the user can review it.

## Workflow

1. Discover the Drive and filesystem tools in this session. Resolve the backup scope: list the folder tree (or run the query) and build a manifest - name, type, size, last-modified, Drive path - before downloading anything.
2. Present the manifest as the review table with total count and estimated size, plus the format conversion per Google-native file. Wait for `apply` before writing anything locally.
3. On apply, mirror the Drive folder structure under the local dated destination (`backup-2026-07-05/Contracts/...`) so files stay findable. Sanitize filenames for the local OS (slashes, colons) and note any renames.
4. Download/export file by file:
   - Google Docs/Sheets/Slides → export in the chosen format; note that comments and version history are NOT carried into exports.
   - Binary files (PDF, images, office files) → download as-is.
   - Shortcuts → skip (they're pointers, not content) and record them in the report.
   - Files that fail (permission, size, API limits) → log and continue; never silently drop.
5. Verify: compare manifest count vs files written; spot-check a few file sizes are non-zero.
6. Deliver the backup report. **Never delete or modify anything in Drive** - this skill copies out; it does not "clean up" the source.

## Output format

```
Backup manifest - <scope>, <n> files, ~<size>
| # | Drive path | Type | Size | Export as | Last modified |
Say `apply` to download to <local path>/backup-<date>/.

--- after apply: Backup report ---
Destination: <path>
Exported: <n>/<total> files (<size>)
Converted: <x> Docs → .docx, <y> Sheets → .xlsx ...
Renamed for filesystem: <list or none>
Skipped: <shortcuts n, oversize m>
FAILED (needs attention): <file → reason, or "none">
Reminder: exports do not include comments or version history.
```

## Do

- Verify @google-drive and @filesystem connectors and discover exact tool names first
- Build and show the manifest before downloading - the user must see scope and size up front
- Preserve the folder structure locally; a flat dump of 400 files is not a usable archive
- Log every failure and every skipped file; a backup that silently misses files is worse than none
- Ask at most ONE clarifying question (usually: which export format for Google-native files?)

## Don't

- Don't delete, move, or "tidy" anything in Drive as part of a backup
- Don't overwrite a previous local backup - always write into a new dated folder unless told otherwise
- Don't export Google-native files without an agreed target format
- Don't claim completeness without comparing written-file count against the manifest
- Don't back up shared files the user only views if scope was "my files" - ownership matters for exit backups

## Quality check (before responding)

- [ ] Output matches requested tone and channel
- [ ] No placeholder brackets left unfilled (use sensible defaults or ask)
- [ ] User can copy-paste the result immediately
- [ ] Required integrations (@google-drive, @filesystem) verified in your AI tool's connector settings before `use_tool` calls
- [ ] Connector actions proposed, not auto-applied (unless read-only)

## Schedule

Recommended cadence: **monthly**

Example: `/loop 7d Run /gdrive-backup-export`
```

After writing the file, confirm it exists. The user can then run /gdrive-backup-export or describe the task from the skill description.

```markdown
---
name: gdrive-contract-finder
description: >
  Find all contracts in Drive. Requires integrations: @google-drive, @word. Uses search_tool + use_tool. Cadence: on-demand. Triggers: "gdrive contract finder", /gdrive-contract-finder.
metadata:
  category: google-drive
  type: connector
  cadence: on-demand
  risk: low
  connectors: google-drive
  integrations: @google-drive,@word
allowed-tools: search_tool, use_tool
---
# /gdrive-contract-finder

Find all contracts in Drive.

## Works with

Use this as a portable `SKILL.md` in Grok, Claude, Cursor, ChatGPT, Codex, VS Code agent skills, Gemini, or any AI workspace that supports reusable markdown instructions. If a tool does not support slash commands, paste the task and ask the AI to follow this skill.

## When to use

"Where is our agreement with <vendor>?" - contracts, NDAs, MSAs, SOWs, and engagement letters are scattered across Drive as Docs, PDFs, and Word attachments saved years apart. Use this to build a contract inventory (due diligence, renewal season, a dispute) or to hunt down one specific agreement and pull its key terms.

## Inputs to gather

Check context first; only ask if missing:

- One contract or a full inventory? If one: counterparty name and rough signing period
- Scope: all of My Drive, specific folders (Legal, Clients), and whether shared drives count
- Contract types in scope: NDA, MSA, SOW, employment, lease, vendor agreements - or everything contract-like
- For inventories: which terms to extract (default: parties, effective date, term/expiry, renewal clause, value)

## Tone & voice

- **Tone:** Link to files. Summarize before long reads.
- **Audience:** Infer from user context.

## Connector access (required)

This skill **cannot run from text alone** - it must call live integrations.
Before any workflow step:

1. Check your AI tool's connector settings and confirm each integration below is enabled and authenticated (Grok: run `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: enable the app in settings · other tools: ask your AI what app connections it has).
2. Use `search_tool` to discover tools for each connector (search by app name).
3. Call integrations with `use_tool` and fully-qualified names (`server__tool_name`).
4. Connector tool names vary by AI tool. Discover the available tools first, then call the exact tool name exposed in the current session.

### Required integrations

- **@word** - Microsoft Word (attach in prompt or enable Office MCP)
  - Local edits: run bundled skill `/docx`

- **@google-drive** - Google Drive
  - MCP server key: `google-drive`
  - Example tools: `google-drive__search_files`, `google-drive__read_file`, `google-drive__create_file`

### If a connector is missing

Stop immediately. Tell the user:

```
This skill needs <@mention> enabled.
1. Open your AI tool's connector/integration settings (Grok: `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: app settings)
2. Enable and authenticate <app>
3. Refresh your tool list, then run this skill again
```

Do **not** pretend you accessed Gmail/Canva/Drive without a successful `use_tool` call.

## Safety & approval

- Separate facts, assumptions, and recommendations.
- Do not give legal, medical, tax, investment, hiring, or security advice as a final authority.
- For sends, publishing, deletes, refunds, account changes, or other irreversible actions, show a review table and wait for explicit approval.

## Workflow

1. Discover the Drive tools, then search in layers and union the results:
   - Title keywords: `contract`, `agreement`, `NDA`, `MSA`, `SOW`, `terms`, `engagement letter`, `lease`, plus the counterparty name if given.
   - Full-text search for signature-page phrases where the connector supports it: "hereinafter", "in witness whereof", "effective date", "governing law".
   - Type filters: PDFs and .docx are where signed contracts hide; a Google Doc is usually a draft.
2. Dedupe and classify each hit by strongest evidence: **signed** (scanned PDF, "executed", signature blocks), **draft** (Doc with tracked changes/comments, "v3_redline" names), **template** (blank party names). Version families like `NDA_Acme_v1/v2/FINAL/FINAL2` group into one row with the likely operative version flagged.
3. For each contract, capture metadata: title, counterparty (from filename or first page), file type, owner, folder path, last modified, and link.
4. If term extraction was requested, open the likely-operative version and pull only the agreed fields (parties, effective date, term/expiry, renewal/notice clause, value). Quote clause text for anything consequential; mark fields "not found" rather than inferring.
5. Highlight risk-relevant findings factually: contracts expiring within 90 days, auto-renewal clauses with notice windows, and "possible duplicate/conflicting versions" families.
6. This is read-only. If the user wants the found contracts moved into a Contracts folder or renamed consistently, propose that as a separate `apply`-gated list.

## Output format

```
Contract inventory - scope: <folders/drives>, searched <date>
Found: <n> contract-like files → <s> signed, <d> drafts, <t> templates

| # | Counterparty | Type | Status | Effective | Expires/Renews | File | Folder |
|---|--------------|------|--------|-----------|----------------|------|--------|
(sorted by expiry date; links on file names)

⏰ Expiring / renewing within 90 days: <list with notice deadlines quoted>
🔀 Version families needing a human decision: "NDA_Acme" - 4 versions, latest signed PDF is v3 but a newer draft exists
❓ Couldn't classify: <list>

Note: term summaries are extracted text, not legal advice - verify clauses in the linked files.
Want these gathered into one folder or renamed consistently? I'll propose the moves for approval.
```

## Do

- Verify the @google-drive connector and discover exact tool names first; use `/docx` or attach @word only when a .docx needs deep reading the Drive connector can't do
- Search full text, not just titles - many signed PDFs are named `scan_20240312.pdf`
- Distinguish signed / draft / template explicitly; an inventory that mixes them is misleading
- Quote the actual renewal or termination clause when flagging a deadline
- Ask at most ONE clarifying question (usually: one contract or full inventory?)

## Don't

- Don't present extracted terms as legal conclusions - separate quoted fact from your reading of it
- Don't assume the newest file is the operative contract; a signed PDF outranks a newer draft
- Don't move, rename, or delete anything during the search - organization is a separate approved step
- Don't skip shared drives if the user's company keeps Legal there - say what was and wasn't searched
- Don't paste entire contracts into chat; quote clauses, link files

## Quality check (before responding)

- [ ] Output matches requested tone and channel
- [ ] No placeholder brackets left unfilled (use sensible defaults or ask)
- [ ] User can copy-paste the result immediately
- [ ] Required integrations (@google-drive, @word) verified in your AI tool's connector settings before `use_tool` calls
- [ ] Connector actions proposed, not auto-applied (unless read-only)
```

After writing the file, confirm it exists. The user can then run /gdrive-contract-finder or describe the task from the skill description.

3. Try clue variants proactively: synonyms ("launch plan" / "go-to-market"), the client's name instead of the project codename, and the file-type flip (the "doc" might be a slide deck).
4. Rank candidates by clue-fit, not just recency: title match > body-phrase match > owner match > timeframe match. Cap at ~7 candidates.
5. For close calls, peek at the first lines of the top candidates to disambiguate ("this one opens with the Q2 OKRs - is that it?"). Only read what disambiguation needs.
6. If confirmation was requested, open the winner and verify it contains the expected content; quote the matching passage.

```

Do **not** pretend you accessed Gmail/Canva/Drive without a successful `use_tool` call.

- Never delete, send, or publish without explicit confirmation.

6. Report the final tree with per-folder counts and every rename performed.

# /gdrive-share-audit

Audit who has access to Drive folders.

## Works with

Use this as a portable `SKILL.md` in Grok, Claude, Cursor, ChatGPT, Codex, VS Code agent skills, Gemini, or any AI workspace that supports reusable markdown instructions. If a tool does not support slash commands, paste the task and ask the AI to follow this skill.

## When to use

Sharing debt accumulates silently: "anyone with the link" files from 2023, an ex-contractor still on the Finance folder, a client with edit rights to internal docs. Run monthly, before an offboarding, or ahead of a compliance/security review to map exactly who can see what - and to propose the revocations worth making.

## Inputs to gather

Check context first; only ask if missing:

- Scope: which folders/drives to audit (default: user-owned folders in My Drive), and whether to recurse into everything or focus on sensitive trees (Finance, HR, Legal, Clients)
- The trust map: internal domain(s), known-partner domains, and any specific people who *should* have access - everything else is "external" by definition
- Special concern: a specific departed person/vendor to hunt for, or a general hygiene sweep
- Appetite: report-only (default) or also propose permission removals for approval

## Tone & voice

- **Tone:** Link to files. Summarize before long reads.
- **Audience:** Infer from user context.

## Connector access (required)

This skill **cannot run from text alone** - it must call live integrations.
Before any workflow step:

1. Check your AI tool's connector settings and confirm each integration below is enabled and authenticated (Grok: run `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: enable the app in settings · other tools: ask your AI what app connections it has).
2. Use `search_tool` to discover tools for each connector (search by app name).
3. Call integrations with `use_tool` and fully-qualified names (`server__tool_name`).
4. Connector tool names vary by AI tool. Discover the available tools first, then call the exact tool name exposed in the current session.

### Required integrations

- **@google-drive** - Google Drive
  - MCP server key: `google-drive`
  - Example tools: `google-drive__search_files`, `google-drive__read_file`, `google-drive__create_file`

### If a connector is missing

Stop immediately. Tell the user:

```
This skill needs <@mention> enabled.
1. Open your AI tool's connector/integration settings (Grok: `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: app settings)
2. Enable and authenticate <app>
3. Refresh your tool list, then run this skill again
```

Do **not** pretend you accessed Gmail/Canva/Drive without a successful `use_tool` call.

## Safety

- The audit itself is **read-only**. Permission removals are writes: present them as a review table and wait for `apply`, `apply all`, or `apply 1,3`.
- Revoking access can break someone's active work - every proposed removal must name who loses what, and removals of currently-active collaborators get an extra warning.

## Workflow

1. Discover the Drive tools and confirm permission metadata is readable in this session (not every Drive connector exposes ACLs - if it doesn't, say so and stop rather than guessing).
2. Walk the in-scope tree and collect, per folder and per sensitive file: owner, direct grantees (user/group, role: viewer/commenter/editor), link-sharing state (restricted / domain / anyone-with-link), and whether access is direct or inherited from a parent. Inheritance matters: one over-shared parent folder can expose an entire tree.
3. Classify every grant against the trust map:
   - 🔴 **High**: "anyone with the link" on sensitive folders; external editors on internal docs; access held by the specifically-named departed person/vendor.
   - 🟠 **Review**: external viewers outside known-partner domains; editor roles that look like they should be viewer; group grants whose membership the user can't see.
   - 🟢 **Expected**: internal domain grants and known partners with sensible roles.
4. Trace each red/orange finding to its source ("inherited from /Clients - fixing the parent fixes 47 files") so fixes land at the right level, not file-by-file.
5. Produce the report; if the user opted into fixes, attach a proposed-revocations table with blast radius per row. Apply only after explicit approval; prefer downgrades (editor→viewer) over removal when collaboration is plausible.
6. After apply, re-read the affected permissions to confirm the change took, and summarize.

## Output format

```
Share audit - scope: <folders>, <n> folders / <m> files checked · trust: internal=<domain>, partners=<list>

🔴 HIGH RISK (<n>)
| # | Item | Exposure | Who/what | Source | Suggested fix |
| 1 | /Finance/2025 | anyone-with-link (editor!) | public link | set on folder directly | restrict to named people |
| 2 | Payroll.xlsx | j.doe@oldvendor.com - editor | direct grant | - | remove (departed vendor) |

🟠 REVIEW (<n>)
| Item | Grant | Question for you |

🟢 Expected access: <k> grants - internal & known partners (list available on request)

Inheritance notes: fixing /Clients parent closes 47 downstream exposures.
Proposed revocations (needs your `apply`):
| # | Remove/downgrade | From | Loses | Last activity known? |
Say `apply`, `apply all`, or `apply 1,3`. Report-only otherwise.
--- after apply ---
Revoked/downgraded <n> grants; verified by re-reading permissions. Failures: <none/list>.
```

## Do

- Verify the @google-drive connector exposes permission data before promising an audit
- Distinguish direct grants from inherited ones and fix at the parent when that's the real source
- Treat "anyone with the link" + editor as the top severity, always - it's writable by the internet
- Name the human impact of every revocation (who loses access to what) before asking for `apply`
- Ask at most ONE clarifying question (usually: which domains count as internal/trusted?)

## Don't

- Don't revoke anything without explicit per-run approval - a broken share during a client deadline is a self-inflicted incident
- Don't mark every external share as a problem; clients on their own project folder is the system working
- Don't overlook group grants - "engineering@" may include contractors; flag groups whose membership is unverifiable
- Don't fix 47 files one by one when one parent-folder change does it - but never change a parent without listing what else inherits from it
- Don't fabricate an ACL you couldn't read; report unreadable items as unaudited

## Quality check (before responding)

- [ ] Output matches requested tone and channel
- [ ] No placeholder brackets left unfilled (use sensible defaults or ask)
- [ ] User can copy-paste the result immediately
- [ ] Required integrations (@google-drive) verified in your AI tool's connector settings before `use_tool` calls
- [ ] Connector actions proposed, not auto-applied (unless read-only)

## Schedule

Recommended cadence: **monthly**

Example: `/loop 7d Run /gdrive-share-audit`
```

```

# /gdrive-to-local-edit

Download Drive file, edit locally, re-upload.

## Works with

Use this as a portable `SKILL.md` in Grok, Claude, Cursor, ChatGPT, Codex, VS Code agent skills, Gemini, or any AI workspace that supports reusable markdown instructions. If a tool does not support slash commands, paste the task and ask the AI to follow this skill.

## When to use

A file in Drive needs edits the Drive connector can't do in place - reformatting a .docx with proper styles, running formulas/cleanup on an .xlsx, batch changes to a .pptx - so the round-trip is: download, edit locally with the right document skill, and put the result back without stomping anyone's concurrent changes.

## Inputs to gather

Check context first; only ask if missing:

- Which file, and the exact edits wanted (the edit spec drives everything else)
- Round-trip mode: overwrite the original as a new version, or upload as a new file alongside (`<name> - edited <date>`); default to new-version for solo files, new-file for actively shared ones
- If it's a Google Doc/Sheet/Slides: converting to Office format and back **loses comments, version history, and some formatting** - confirm the user accepts that, or steer to in-place Drive edits instead
- Local working directory for the temporary copy

## Tone & voice

- **Tone:** Link to files. Summarize before long reads.
- **Audience:** Infer from user context.

## Connector access (required)

This skill **cannot run from text alone** - it must call live integrations.
Before any workflow step:

1. Check your AI tool's connector settings and confirm each integration below is enabled and authenticated (Grok: run `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: enable the app in settings · other tools: ask your AI what app connections it has).
2. Use `search_tool` to discover tools for each connector (search by app name).
3. Call integrations with `use_tool` and fully-qualified names (`server__tool_name`).
4. Connector tool names vary by AI tool. Discover the available tools first, then call the exact tool name exposed in the current session.

### Required integrations

- **@google-drive** - Google Drive
  - MCP server key: `google-drive`
  - Example tools: `google-drive__search_files`, `google-drive__read_file`, `google-drive__create_file`

- **@filesystem** - Local filesystem
  - MCP server key: `filesystem`
  - Example tools: `filesystem__read_file`, `filesystem__write_file`, `filesystem__list_directory`

### If a connector is missing

Stop immediately. Tell the user:

```
This skill needs <@mention> enabled.
1. Open your AI tool's connector/integration settings (Grok: `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: app settings)
2. Enable and authenticate <app>
3. Refresh your tool list, then run this skill again
```

Do **not** pretend you accessed Gmail/Canva/Drive without a successful `use_tool` call.

## Safety

- **Propose before write**: show a review table; wait for user to say `apply`, `apply all`, or `apply 1,3`.
- Never delete, send, or publish without explicit confirmation.
- Summarize every action taken at the end of the run so the user can review it.

## Workflow

1. Discover the Drive and filesystem tools. Locate the file and record its pre-flight state: file ID, exact name, MIME type, **last-modified time and last editor**, and sharing status - this snapshot is the collision detector for step 5.
2. Warn before conversion if it's a Google-native file: exporting to .docx/.xlsx/.pptx sheds comments, suggestions, and revision history; proceed only after the user acknowledges (or reroute simple edits to the Drive connector's in-place editing).
3. Download to the working directory. Edit the local copy with the matching document skill (`/docx`, `/xlsx`, `/pptx` where bundled - otherwise the session's document tools), making **only** the requested edits; resist drive-by reformatting.
4. Present the review before any upload: a concise change list (sections touched, rows/cells altered, slides changed) and, where practical, before/after snippets. Wait for `apply`.
5. Pre-upload collision check: re-read the Drive file's modified time. If it changed since step 1, someone edited during the round-trip - **stop**, report who/when, and offer choices (re-do edits on the fresh copy, or upload as a separate file) rather than overwriting their work.
6. Upload per the agreed mode: new-version keeps the same file ID so links keep working; new-file uploads alongside with a dated name. Verify the upload (size/modified time), then report and offer to clean up the local temp copy.

## Output format

```
Round-trip plan - <file name> (<type>, last edited <when> by <who>)
Mode: <new version | new file "<name> - edited 2026-07-05">
⚠ Conversion note (Google-native only): comments & history won't survive the trip.

--- after local edit: Review ---
Changes made locally:
| # | Where | Change |
| 1 | §2 Pricing | table reformatted, 3 rows added |
| 2 | Footer | version bumped to v1.3 |
Before/after: <snippet where practical>
Say `apply` to upload.

--- after apply ---
Collision check: no edits on Drive since download ✅ (or: ⚠ Priya edited at 14:02 - upload halted, options below)
Uploaded as <mode>; link: <url>. Local temp copy at <path> - delete it?
```

## Do

- Verify @google-drive and @filesystem connectors and discover exact tool names first
- Snapshot modified-time before download and re-check it before upload - the silent-overwrite race is this workflow's one fatal bug
- Keep the same file ID (new version) when others hold links to the doc
- Make only the requested edits; list every change in the review
- Delete or offer to delete the local temp copy after a successful round-trip

## Don't

- Don't upload over a file that changed mid-round-trip - stop and surface the conflict
- Don't convert Google-native files without the explicit loss warning
- Don't upload before the user's `apply` on the change review
- Don't leave sensitive downloaded copies lying in local temp folders unmentioned
- Don't rename the file or move it to another folder as an upload side effect

## Quality check (before responding)

- [ ] Output matches requested tone and channel
- [ ] No placeholder brackets left unfilled (use sensible defaults or ask)
- [ ] User can copy-paste the result immediately
- [ ] Required integrations (@google-drive, @filesystem) verified in your AI tool's connector settings before `use_tool` calls
- [ ] Connector actions proposed, not auto-applied (unless read-only)
```
